overview: Replaces characters that are invalid in XML by a configurable marker string. The step processes raw documents, recognizing both raw invalid characters and numeric character references (NCRs) such as `&#000B;` or ``. Output encoding matches the input encoding, and Byte-Order-Marks are preserved if present.

replacement.help:
The string used to replace each invalid XML character. Must be a valid [Java Formatter string](http://download.oracle.com/javase/6/docs/api/java/util/Formatter.html). The formatter always receives the **integer value of the Unicode code-point** as its argument.

To **remove** invalid characters entirely, leave this field empty.

Some characters have special meaning in Java Formatter syntax and may need escaping — for example, literal `%` must be written as `%%`.
replacement.values: A Java `Formatter` pattern string. The single format argument is the integer Unicode code-point value. Use `%X` for uppercase hex, `%d` for decimal, `%03X` for zero-padded hex, etc. Literal `%` must be escaped as `%%`.
examples: `_#x%X;` — replaces U+000B with `_#xB;` (XML-like NCR with underscore prefix)
`?` — replaces any invalid character with a literal question mark
`�` — replaces with the Unicode replacement character NCR
`%%%d%%` — replaces U+000B with `%11%` (decimal value wrapped in literal percent signs)

processingNotes: Input encoding must be specified unless the file has a Byte-Order-Mark (BOM).
Output is written in the same encoding as the input document.
Byte-Order-Marks are preserved if present in the input.
The step recognizes both raw invalid characters and numeric character references (NCRs) such as `&#000B;` or ``.

examples.title: Replace invalid characters with XML-style NCR markers
examples.description: Uses the default replacement string to convert invalid XML characters into underscore-prefixed hex NCRs, making them visible and parseable.

examples.title: Remove invalid characters
examples.description: Leaving the replacement string empty causes all invalid XML characters to be silently removed from the document.
